description:
You are working as an observability engineer who is tidying up the local environment variables used by a handful of home-grown monitoring dashboards.  
Set up a tiny “dotenv” workspace so that the dashboards can load their configuration in a reproducible way.  
  
Target workspace  
• Directory that must exist at the end: **/home/user/observability/env/** (create intermediate levels if they do not already exist).  
  
Files that must exist inside that directory when you are done  
1. **/home/user/observability/env/.env** – the canonical dotenv file.  
   • Plain‐text UTF-8.  
   • Exactly three non-comment lines (no leading or trailing blank lines).  
   • The lines must appear in **this exact order**:  
     1. PROMETHEUS_URL=http://prometheus.internal:9090  
     2. GRAFANA_URL=http://grafana.internal:3000  
     3. DASHBOARD_REFRESH_RATE=30s  
   • A single trailing newline character after the last line is required.  
   • No spaces before or after the “=” sign.  
2. **/home/user/observability/env/env_load.log** – a one-shot log that proves the variables were successfully exported into your current shell.  
   • Plain‐text UTF-8.  
   • Must contain exactly three lines, sorted alphabetically by the variable name, each line in the form  
     VAR_NAME=VAR_VALUE  
   • Therefore the file content (and nothing else) must be:  
     DASHBOARD_REFRESH_RATE=30s  
     GRAFANA_URL=http://grafana.internal:3000  
     PROMETHEUS_URL=http://prometheus.internal:9090  
   • A single trailing newline character after the last line is required.  
  
Task to perform  
1. Create the directory hierarchy if it does not already exist.  
2. Create and populate the .env file exactly as specified.  
3. Load (export) the variables into your current shell session **from the .env file**, then immediately generate env_load.log precisely as described above. A common pattern is to source the file and use a command such as `printenv`/`env`/`grep`/`sort` combined with I/O redirection, but you may choose any approach that yields the required file with the correct content.  
  
Constraints & verification rules  
• Use only standard Unix utilities available on a minimal Debian/Ubuntu-style system.  
• Do **not** hard-code paths in the log beyond the directory specified.  
• The automated checker will verify:  
  – that /home/user/observability/env/ exists and is a directory;  
  – that .env and env_load.log exist as regular files;  
  – that the byte-for-byte contents of each file exactly match the specifications above (including trailing newline).  
• No other output files are necessary.  
• Keep the command sequence short (roughly two or three commands in total is enough).  
• When you are done, you may display the contents of env_load.log to the console as human confirmation, but it is not mandatory.
truth:
Directory expected: /home/user/observability/env/

File #1
Path: /home/user/observability/env/.env
Exact content (including final newline):
PROMETHEUS_URL=http://prometheus.internal:9090
GRAFANA_URL=http://grafana.internal:3000
DASHBOARD_REFRESH_RATE=30s
[EOF with single trailing newline]

File #2
Path: /home/user/observability/env/env_load.log
Exact content (including final newline):
DASHBOARD_REFRESH_RATE=30s
GRAFANA_URL=http://grafana.internal:3000
PROMETHEUS_URL=http://prometheus.internal:9090
[EOF with single trailing newline]
